reasoning: The FIDE rating listed on Wikipedia for Nakamura on March 16, 2025, is 2802. A 1% increase means he would need an official FIDE rating of at least 2830.02. In classical chess history, only four players have ever officially crossed 2830 on a FIDE list (Carlsen, Kasparov, Caruana, Aronian). Nakamura's peak FIDE rating is 2816. At the 2800 level, gaining 28 rating points requires an extraordinarily dominant tournament performance (e.g., scoring +6 or more unbeaten against top-tier Super GMs), which is extremely implausible in modern top-level chess, particularly given Nakamura's relatively lower frequency of classical chess events compared to his prime, as well as his focus on streaming and online rapid/blitz formats. Additionally, Wikipedia updates the top 100 FIDE rankings only once a month matching FIDE's official monthly release. Thus, the near-term dates (March 23, April 15) have functionally 0% chance of reflecting such a massive rating spike, as there are neither the events nor the mathematical opportunity for such an increase. Even extending the horizon to March 2026, achieving a 2830+ rating is exceedingly unlikely. I am setting the probabilities effectively to the minimum permitted limit.
